_meta.purpose: Platform-specific prompt notes and technical specs for AI video/image generation in the anime MV pipeline.
_meta.usage: video_prompt_generator.py appends the 'note' field to each scene's video_prompt. image_platforms are used by image_prompt_generator for image generation.

platforms.prompt_structure_note: Lead with camera type, then describe the scene. Reinforce key ideas in multiple sections. Use positive descriptions only — never 'the camera does not move'.

camera_control_params.description: Gen-3 Alpha Turbo has a slider-based camera control UI (separate from text prompts)

style_tips: Lead with camera type before any other description
Use specific cinematic terms: 'diffused lighting', 'silhouette', 'backlighting', 'iridescent'
Reinforce key concepts in multiple sections of the prompt
Avoid incompatible aesthetic mixes (e.g., modern + medieval)
Use temporal language: 'slow motion', 'hyperspeed', 'timelapse' for pacing
For anime MV: specify 'anime cel-shading', 'neon-lit', 'vibrant color palette' early

parameters.motion_strength: Controlled via camera control sliders (-10 to 10) or text descriptors

forbidden_terms: Avoid negative phrasing ('the camera doesn't move', 'no motion')
Avoid combining contradictory aesthetic styles in one prompt
platforms.max_length: No official limit stated; keep under 200 words for best adherence
platforms.note: Use image-to-video mode when a reference frame exists. Lead with '[camera type]:' before any other description. Reinforce key visual ideas in multiple sections. Keep motion subtle and cinematic for anime MV atmosphere.

platforms.prompt_structure_note: 4-part core: Subject + Action + Context (3-5 elements) + Style. Use natural descriptive language, not keyword lists. Always end motion with a resolution state ('settles back into place') to prevent generation hangs.

model_variants.kling_1.6_standard: 30-50 words. Single subject, one action, 2-3 context elements. Has Motion Brush for manual control.
model_variants.kling_2.5_turbo_pro: 40-60 words. Max 3-4 environmental elements. Single straightforward camera move. Recommended default.
model_variants.kling_2.6: 60-100 words. 5-7 environmental elements. Detailed lighting. Nuanced expressions.
model_variants.kling_o1: 50-100+ words. Complex multi-step camera + editing operations. Explicit verbs: swap, replace, remove.

platforms.image_to_video_note: For I2V: focus exclusively on motion (15-40 words). Never redescribe existing image content. Specify what moves vs. what stays static.
style_tips: Specify speed of camera: 'slow dolly', 'rapid pan', 'gentle tilt'
Define motion start and end: 'pan from left to right across the city'
Always include a motion conclusion: 'settles back', 'comes to rest', 'returns to stillness'
Use spatial language: 'positioned near', 'making contact with', 'gripping', 'traveling toward'
For character consistency, anchor distinctive traits: 'red jacket with blue scarf'
Use verbs/adverbs for motion quality: 'aggressively accelerates' beats 'drives fast'
Lighting: sunlit, golden hour, neon glow, dramatic stage lighting, moonlit

parameters.motion_strength: Controlled via descriptors ('gentle', 'rapid', 'dramatic') or Motion Brush (1.6)

platforms.max_length: 30-60 words optimal. 60-100 for Kling 2.6/O1. Over 100 risks processing failures.
platforms.note: Prioritize consistent character identity, clear camera motion, and restrained atmospheric movement. Use the 4-part formula: [shot_type] of [subject+action], [environment], [camera move], [lighting+style]. Always end with a motion conclusion state. Keep to 40-60 words for Kling 2.5 Turbo Pro.

platforms.prompt_structure_note: Describe scene naturally; use -camera parameter for camera move. Parameters use dash-flag syntax appended after prompt text.

camera_syntax.constraint: Only one camera move type at a time (zoom, pan, rotate are mutually exclusive)

style_tips: Use short clips (5-8s) for cleanest motion results
Combine -motion 2-3 for anime-style energetic movement
Use -neg to suppress: 'blurry, distorted faces, extra limbs, watermark, flickering'
For anime: include 'anime style, cel-shaded, vibrant colors, clean lines' in prompt
Keep description concise — Pika interprets camera directions from text too
platforms.max_length: No official limit; concise prompts (1-3 sentences) work best
platforms.note: Use shorter clips (5-8s) with simple, direct motion instructions. Append -motion 2 for anime energy, -neg 'blurry, flickering, distorted' to clean artifacts. Specify camera moves with -camera [direction]. Keep end-state clean for smooth transitions between scenes.

platforms.prompt_structure_note: 3-4 sentences for best results. Use conversational natural language. Camera is controlled purely through text — type 'camera' in prompt to trigger dropdown of 12 supported moves.
platforms.camera_api_note: API: call Camera Motions endpoint to get array of supported camera motion strings. Use exact strings like 'camera orbit left' in prompt.
style_tips: Use style preset tags: 'Anime', 'Cinematic' for consistent looks
Emphasize depth, parallax, and environmental atmosphere for cinematic feel
Include mood descriptors: 'minimalist', 'surreal', 'watercolor' — reuse across prompts for consistency
Combine: '4k, photorealistic, anamorphic lens, film grain, bokeh, depth of field'
Use '@character' after uploading reference image for character consistency
Use '@style' with image upload for style reference
For anime MV: 'anime, song-matched limited palette, expressive lighting, atmospheric depth'

parameters.duration: Configurable; use Extend feature for longer sequences
parameters.motion_strength: Controlled via text descriptors and camera motion strings

platforms.max_length: 3-4 sentences recommended; no strict character limit
platforms.note: Emphasize camera path, depth, parallax, and environmental atmosphere. Use 'camera orbit left/right', 'cinematic push-in', or 'drone shot' for expressive motion. Include style tag 'Anime' or 'Cinematic' for consistent visual language. Reference images via @character or @style for continuity across scenes.

platforms.prompt_structure: [scene description: characters, costumes, setting, weather]. [cinematography: camera shot, framing, depth of field, lens/style, mood]. [actions: specific beats or gestures]. [dialogue if any]. [sound/audio cues].
platforms.prompt_structure_note: 5-section structure. Not every section is required — leaving elements open-ended gives Sora creative freedom. One clear camera move + one clear subject action per shot.
camera_keywords: aerial wide shot, slight downward angle
medium close-up, slight angle from behind
style_tips: Set aesthetic anchor early: '1970s film', 'IMAX-scale', '16mm black-and-white', 'anime cel-shaded'
Replace vague words: 'wet asphalt, zebra crosswalk, neon reflection' beats 'a beautiful street'
Describe dialogue in a labeled block below prose: 'Character A says: ...'
Keep motion simple: one camera move + one subject action per shot
Use beats for timing: 'Takes four steps, pauses, pulls curtain' (not 'walks across room')
For complex shots: specify lensing, filtration, lighting, grading, and motion in production terms
Shorter clips (4-8s) have better adherence; stitch multiple shots instead of one long clip
Use 'sora-2-pro' model for higher resolution options

parameters.character_references: Up to 2 uploaded character reference images

forbidden_terms: Avoid vague aesthetic words: 'beautiful', 'cinematic look', 'amazing'
Replace with observable specifics: colors, materials, actions, measurements
platforms.max_length: No official limit; concise and specific beats long and vague
platforms.note: Favor a concise cinematic prompt with clear subject, camera motion, lighting, and temporal continuity. Use the 5-section format: scene / cinematography / actions / dialogue / sound. Set aesthetic style early ('anime', '2D animation', 'neon-lit'). Keep to one camera move and one subject action per shot. Avoid adding extra characters or unrelated visual ideas.

platforms.prompt_structure_note: Narrative-driven: write as a director's script, not a keyword list. Use specific verbs and adverbs for motion quality. The LLM backbone responds to temporal narrative flow.
style_tips: Write narrative prose, not comma-separated keywords
Good: 'A cyberpunk samurai sprints desperately through a rainy Neo-Tokyo market'
Bad: 'Cyberpunk city, rain, neon, 4k, running'
Define anchor features for character consistency: 'red jacket with blue scarf'
Use strong verbs/adverbs: 'aggressively accelerates, careening' beats 'drives fast'
Include weather, lighting, time of day, architectural details for environmental richness
Add basic camera moves (dolly, zoom) then advanced (crane, tracking) for sophistication
Multi-modal: combine text with reference images for character/style consistency

parameters.motion_strength: Controlled via descriptive verbs and adverbs

platforms.negative_prompt_note: Use negative prompts to eliminate unwanted elements
platforms.max_length: No strict limit; use enough detail to specify all scene elements; avoid over-complication
platforms.note: Keep action simple and readable. Emphasize stable anime identity, controlled motion, and clean neon atmosphere. Write as narrative prose: '[camera move] [character] [action] in [scene], [lighting], [style]'. Use strong action verbs for motion quality. Include anchor traits for character consistency.

platforms.prompt_structure: [subject with defining characteristics] [specific action or motion] [environment/setting] with [visual style], [cinematography], and [mood/atmosphere].
platforms.prompt_structure_note: 5 core elements: subject, action/motion, environment, lighting/mood, style. Include negative prompt separately to suppress artifacts. Style presets are selectable in UI.
style_tips: Use style presets for consistent aesthetic: 'anime' for MV work
Name shot type, camera move, and pacing explicitly in prompt
Use motion verbs for video: 'slow pan', 'tracking shot', 'dynamic action'
Add reference images for visual guidance
Avoid generic/ambiguous language; be specific about subject characteristics
Lock seeds for refining successful generations
Use multi-clip generation for dynamic shot transitions
Include audio switch for auto BGM/SFX if available
platforms.max_length: Balance length with clarity; overly long prompts confuse the model
platforms.note: Use image-to-video when possible. Use 'anime' style preset for MV work. Keep camera movement direct — 'camera slowly pushes in', 'tracking shot follows subject'. Avoid overcomplicated effects. Preserve the established color palette. Use negative prompt: 'blurry, distorted faces, extra limbs, watermark, flickering'.

platforms.prompt_structure_example: Medium shot, a tired corporate worker, rubbing his temples in exhaustion, in front of a bulky 1980s computer in a cluttered office late at night. The scene is lit by harsh fluorescent overhead lights. Retro aesthetic, shot as if on 1980s color film, slightly grainy.
style_tips: Lead with cinematography: shot type and camera movement first
Specify visual medium explicitly: 'cartoon', 'claymation', 'film noir on 35mm', 'anime 2D'
Use sensory/evocative language for immersive world-building
For action sequences: provide play-by-play directions with timing cues
Use timestamp prompting for multi-segment clips: '[00:00-00:02] subject walks in'
Dialogue: use quotation marks: 'A woman says, "We have to leave now."'
SFX: 'SFX: thunder cracks in the distance'
Ambient: 'Ambient noise: quiet hum of a starship bridge'
Negative prompts: describe exclusions: 'no buildings or roads'
Keep prompts concise for basic shots; detailed for complex cinematography
All Veo output is watermarked with SynthID

advanced_features.image_to_video: Animate source images with prompt adherence
advanced_features.ingredients_to_video: Provide reference images for consistent characters/styles
advanced_features.first_last_frame: Generate transitions between start and end images
advanced_features.add_remove_object: Insert/delete objects from existing videos (Veo 2, no audio)
advanced_features.gemini_enhancement: Use Gemini 2.5 Flash to enrich simple prompts before sending to Veo

parameters.watermark: SynthID watermark applied to all outputs

platforms.max_length: Keep concise for basic shots; detailed prompts are supported for complex cinematic work
platforms.note: Use the highest quality cinematic mode available. Lead with cinematography: '[shot type], [subject], [action], [context]. [Style].' Keep the shot coherent, physically plausible, and faithful to the reference image. Use 'anime 2D', 'cel-shaded', and the song-matched limited palette for MV style. Leverage image-to-video and first/last frame features for seamless scene transitions.

platforms.prompt_structure_note: Flow is a scene-building workflow tool built on Veo. Emphasis on multi-scene consistency, character continuity, and shot sequencing within a project.
style_tips: Preserve shot intent and character continuity across generated clips
Maintain the fixed channel color palette across all scenes
Use consistent character descriptors in every scene prompt
Leverage Flow's scene management for storyboard sequencing
Combine with Veo image-to-video and Ingredients-to-Video for reference consistency
platforms.note: Treat as a scene-building prompt for Google Flow/Veo workflows. Preserve shot intent, character continuity, and the fixed channel palette across generated clips. Follow the same prompt structure as Veo. Emphasize scene-to-scene consistency within the MV arc.

platforms.prompt_structure: [shot_size], [camera_angle], [composition]. [Subject description] in [scene description]. [Subject motion with speed]. [Camera movement]. [Lighting type], [time_of_day], [color_tone]. [Art style/visual effect].
platforms.prompt_structure_note: 80-120 words optimal for T2V. I2V(Image-to-Video) 모드: 캐릭터 설명 생략하고 모션/카메라만 기술. 주체 모션과 카메라 모션을 분리해서 명시.
camera_keywords: camera pushes in for a close-up
style_tips: Use '2D anime style' or 'Chinese animation style' explicitly in the style slot
Describe subject motion and camera motion separately
Add speed modifiers: 'slowly', 'gently', 'smoothly', 'quickly', 'gradually'
For I2V: focus prompt on motion only — omit appearance details already in the image
Keep one main scene and one main action per clip for best coherence
Time of day and lighting type significantly affect mood — always specify
Guidance scale: 7.0–7.5 for fast motion; steps: 20–30
Use 720p for quality, 480p for fast iteration
platforms.max_length: 80-120 words for T2V; shorter for I2V (motion description only)
platforms.note: Use T2V for full scene generation; switch to I2V when character appearance must match a reference image. Structure: '[shot size], [camera angle]. [character + scene]. [subject motion]. [camera movement]. [lighting]. [2D anime style]'. Specify motion and camera separately. Always include negative prompt to suppress artifacts.

platforms.prompt_structure:
Composition: `[ComponentName]`
Purpose: [scene goal]
Scene Data: [key fields]
Layer Plan: [background, subject, motif, foreground, mask]
Animation Plan: [frame mapping, easing, keyframes]
Implementation Notes: [React/Remotion constraints]
platforms.prompt_structure_note: Remotion prompts are not video generation prompts — they are implementation briefs for React/Remotion composition code. Structure as a developer spec, not a creative description.

key_apis.useCurrentFrame: Returns current frame number starting at 0
key_apis.spring: spring({fps, frame, damping}) — spring-based motion 0 to 1

animation_patterns.parallax: translateX with interpolate at different rates per layer
animation_patterns.beat_pulse: Short accent pulses mapped to beat timestamps
animation_patterns.camera_push: scale + translateX/Y via interpolate for dolly/push effect

layer_convention: 2. Primary subject / reference image (stable, minimal transform)
3. Motif layer (particles, reflections, waveform lights, memory fragments)
5. Optional mask layer (wipes, reflection reveals, object-led transitions)
style_tips: Map first third to establishing motion, middle to main action, final third to transition prep
Animate transforms, light, and atmosphere MORE than facial/body redesign
Use easing for slow sections; short accent pulses for beat peaks
Build with React, absolute-positioned layers, CSS transforms, Img, SVG/CSS masks
Avoid readable text inside frame unless manually required
Use random('seed') NOT Math.random() for deterministic render
Use Sequence and Series for timeline beats; TransitionSeries for cross-fades
platforms.note: Convert the scene into a Remotion-friendly composition plan with timeline beats, layers, keyframe animation, camera transforms, and reusable props. Structure as: Composition name / Scene Data / Layer Plan / Animation Plan / Implementation Notes. Use useCurrentFrame() + interpolate() for all motion. Keep colors, subject, and camera intent faithful to the matching image prompt. Build with React, Remotion, CSS transforms, masks, gradients, and image layers.

image_platforms.model_status: gpt-image-2 is current default. DALL-E 3 deprecated May 2026. Use gpt-image-2 or gpt-image-1.5.
image_platforms.prompt_structure: [background/scene] + [subject] + [key details: materials, textures, lighting] + [constraints: exclusions, preserved elements] + [intended use / output mode].
image_platforms.prompt_structure_note: Be concrete about materials, shapes, textures. Include visual medium (photo, watercolor, 3D render, anime cel). Add targeted quality levers only when necessary.
style_tips: Specify framing and viewpoint: 'close-up', 'wide', 'top-down', 'eye-level'
Define perspective and lighting: 'soft diffuse', 'golden hour', 'high-contrast neon'
For people: describe scale, body framing, gaze direction, action interactions
For text in images: place literal text in QUOTES or ALL CAPS; spell out tricky words letter-by-letter
State exclusions: 'no watermark', 'no extra text', 'no logos/trademarks'
Use 'preserve' language: 'keep everything else the same'
For anime: 'anime cel-shaded illustration, vibrant saturated colors, clean linework, detailed background'
For photorealism: 'photorealistic', 'real photograph', 'iPhone photo'; emphasize texture/imperfection
Iterate with small single-change follow-ups; re-specify critical details if drift occurs

parameters.n: Integer — number of variations per call

forbidden_terms: No watermarks
No unintended logos or trademarks
No copyrighted characters
No extra text beyond specified copy
No generic stock-photo treatment for professional work
image_platforms.max_length: No official limit; 1-3 detailed paragraphs work well
image_platforms.note: Use for scene reference frames and character model sheets. Use gpt-image-2 at quality='high' for detailed anime scenes. Specify 'anime cel-shaded illustration, vibrant saturated colors, clean linework' for style consistency. Include 'no watermark, no logos, no extra text' in every prompt. Use 1536x1024 for landscape MV frames, 1024x1536 for portrait/vertical.

image_platforms.platform_note: 사용자가 '나노바나 프로'로 부르는 Google Gemini 이미지 생성 플랫폼. Imagen 3 모델 기반.
image_platforms.prompt_structure: 자연어 설명. 특수 문법 없음. 대화형 멀티턴 편집 지원.
image_platforms.prompt_structure_note: 복잡한 프롬프트 엔지니어링 불필요. 원하는 것을 자연스럽게 설명. 한국어, 영어, 일본어 모두 지원. 이전 대화 맥락 유지.
style_tips: 자연스럽게 작성: '저녁노을 아래 설산에 캐릭터를 배치하고 극적인 구름을 추가해줘'
한국어 프롬프트 네이티브 지원
캐릭터 일관성 우수 — 한 번 설명하면 여러 장면에서 유지
텍스트 렌더링: 이미지 내 텍스트는 따옴표 사용
대화형 편집: '더 극적으로', '비 추가', '밤 하늘로 변경' 등 자연어 수정 가능
사진-애니 변환: '애니 셀 쉐이딩 스타일, 비비드 컬러로 변환해줘'
배경 교체, 낮↔밤 변환, 스타일 이전 모두 지원
For anime MV: '일본 애니메이션 셀 쉐이딩, 선명한 색감, 깔끔한 선화, 시네마틱 배경'
씬 이미지 생성 후 '캐릭터 동일하게 유지하면서 배경만 바꿔줘' 식으로 연속 편집 가능
forbidden_terms: 저작권 있는 캐릭터 직접 지정 금지
SynthID 워터마크 자동 적용됨
image_platforms.max_length: 제한 없음. 자연스러운 문장으로 충분히 설명.
image_platforms.note: Google Gemini (Imagen 3) 이미지 생성. 한국어 프롬프트 직접 사용 가능. 자연어로 작성하며 특수 문법 불필요. 캐릭터 일관성과 대화형 반복 편집이 강점. MV 파이프라인에서 씬 레퍼런스 이미지, 캐릭터 시트, 환경 컨셉아트 생성에 활용. 비율은 16:9 (가로형 MV 프레임), 9:16 (세로형/숏폼) 권장.

image_platforms.prompt_structure_note: 파라미터는 항상 프롬프트 텍스트 뒤에. 파라미터 사이 쉼표 없음. V7 기본값: 더 사실적인 텍스처, 해부학적 오류 40% 감소. 단, 그루지/다크 스타일은 V6.1이 더 적합.
style_tips: Parameters always AFTER text — no punctuation between parameters
Use --s 250-400 for artistic/painterly anime feel; --s 100 for balanced
Multi-prompt weights: `concept A::3 concept B::1` (double colon + weight)
Anime style on V7: use --niji 7 (see Nijijourney entry) for best anime results
Character consistency: --oref [URL] (Omni Reference) + --ow 100-500
Style reference: --sref [URL] + --sw 100-500
Get seed for reproducibility: react with envelope emoji to any generation
Text in images: wrap in 'quotes' — still unreliable for complex text
For cinematic anime on V7: 'cinematic anime style, detailed background, dramatic lighting, film grain, 35mm' --v 7

parameters.--v 7: Current default model. Better anatomy, materials, prompt understanding.
parameters.--no: Negative prompt: --no watermark, text, logo, blur
parameters.--oref: Omni Reference — subject/character transfer from URL

forbidden_terms: Don't mix parameters with commas between them
Avoid copyrighted character names
Avoid 'no X' in text — use --no X parameter instead
image_platforms.max_length: No strict limit; concise prompts work well. Long prompts supported with multi-prompt weights.
image_platforms.note: For anime MV frames, prefer Nijijourney (--niji 7) entry below. For cinematic/mixed styles on V7: include 'cinematic anime style, dramatic lighting' + --v 7 --ar 16:9 --s 300 --no watermark, text, logo. Use --oref for character consistency. Draft mode for fast iteration.

image_platforms.prompt_structure_note: Niji 7 (2026-01-09 출시): 프롬프트를 문자 그대로 해석. 배경을 명시하지 않으면 최소/단순 배경 생성. --cref 미지원 (캐릭터 레퍼런스 시스템 재구축 중). 탐색보다 정밀 실행에 최적.
style_tips: Niji 7 is STRICT — describe EVERYTHING explicitly, it won't add context
Background: must describe in detail, or will be blank/minimal
Best for: character key visuals, scene stills, consistent anime style
Style anchors: 'anime screenshot', 'anime key visual', 'anime cel shading', 'cinematic anime', 'anime film still'
Keep --chaos low (0–6) for consistency; Niji 7 default is already low variation
Use --s 100–300 for anime balance; higher for artistic freedom
--sref + URL works better in Niji 7 for style consistency
For this anime MV pipeline: 'anime key visual, cinematic composition, song-matched limited palette, detailed background'
Niji 6 legacy style params (still valid for --niji 6): --style cute / scenic / expressive / original

parameters.--niji 6: Legacy anime model (--style cute/scenic/expressive/original still work)
parameters.--chaos: 0–6 for consistency; higher causes incoherence in anime
parameters.--sref: Style reference URL — works well in Niji 7

forbidden_terms: --cref not supported in Niji 7 (character reference system rebuilt)
Don't assume background — describe explicitly
Old Niji 6 style prompting (vague, short) will produce minimal results in Niji 7
image_platforms.max_length: Detailed prompts work well in Niji 7. Fully describe character traits, clothing, background.
image_platforms.note: 이 파이프라인의 기본 이미지 플랫폼으로 권장. 씬 이미지 프롬프트에 '--niji 7 --ar 16:9 --s 250 --no watermark, text, logo' 추가. 배경 반드시 상세 기술. 캐릭터 일관성은 --seed + 상세 외형 설명으로 유지. anime key visual / anime film still 스타일 앵커 사용.

image_platforms.prompt_structure_note: 자연어 문장으로 작성. 키워드 나열 금지. 가중치 문법 (concept:1.5) 무효. 부정 프롬프트 미지원 (dev/schnell). 40-75 단어 최적.

model_variants.FLUX.1 [schnell]: 1-4 steps. Apache 2.0 license. 40-50 words max. Local deployment.

style_tips: WRONG: '(anime girl:1.5), masterpiece, best quality' → RIGHT: 'An anime girl with long blue hair standing in a rain-soaked city street'
NO negative prompts (dev/schnell) — describe what you WANT, not what to avoid
NO prompt weights — use 'with emphasis on X' or 'prominently featuring X' instead
Text in images: wrap in double quotes: a sign reading "OPEN NOW"
Color precision: use hex codes #FF5733 for exact colors
Layered description: foreground → middle ground → background order helps coherence
ComfyUI dual input: clip_l (short keywords) + t5xxl (detailed sentence)
Anime keywords: 'anime style, cel-shading, 2D anime illustration, cinematic anime, key visual'
Artist references work: 'in the style of Makoto Shinkai'
Do NOT use 'white background' in dev — causes blurriness

comfyui_params.resolution: Width/height must be multiples of 32, range 256–1440px

forbidden_terms: No (concept:1.5) weight syntax — does nothing
No negative prompts in dev/schnell
No white background in dev — causes blurriness
No keyword lists — write natural sentences only
image_platforms.max_length: 40-75 words for dev/pro; 40-50 words max for schnell
image_platforms.note: 자연어 문장으로 작성. 키워드 나열 없이 묘사형 문장 사용. 'anime style, cel-shading, 2D anime illustration, cinematic key visual' 스타일 앵커 포함. 가중치 문법/부정 프롬프트 사용 금지. ComfyUI 사용 시 clip_l에 짧은 키워드, t5xxl에 상세 문장 입력.

image_platforms.prompt_structure_note: Plain natural language. Phoenix는 긴 복합 문장을 잘 처리. 짧은 키워드 나열보다 상세 문장 권장. Alchemy 파이프라인으로 해상도/조명 품질 대폭 향상.

model_variants.Phoenix 1.0: 프롬프트 준수율 높음, 텍스트 렌더링, 고충실도
model_variants.Phoenix 2.0: 최신 (2026). Consistent Character Engine, LoRA 통합, 2048×2048 네이티브

style_tips: Use Alchemy pipeline for final outputs (higher resolution, better lighting/contrast)
Style presets via API: 'CINEMATIC', 'DYNAMIC' — avoid generic 'Anime' preset (known distortion issues)
Character consistency: Character Reference (ID: 397) + Fixed Seed combination
Phoenix 2.0: LoRA (style) + Consistent Character Engine (identity) — unique advantage
Image Guidance types: Style Reference (166), Character Reference (397), Content Reference (364), Pose to Image, Edge to Image, Depth to Image
guidance_scale 7 for strict prompt adherence; 3-5 for creative latitude
Anime style: 'cel-shading, vibrant colors, large expressive eyes, clean linework, dynamic poses'
Avoid generic 'Anime' style preset — it produces distorted results
Train custom LoRA in under 15 min with 10+ reference images for specific anime aesthetic

parameters.alchemy: on/off — premium pipeline, significantly better quality

image_guidance_types.Character Reference (397): Subject likeness; humanoids only; one image only

image_platforms.max_length: No strict limit; Phoenix handles long complex prompts well
image_platforms.note: 캐릭터 일관성이 강점. Phoenix 2.0 + Consistent Character Engine + LoRA 조합으로 MV 전체 씬의 캐릭터 통일성 유지 가능. Alchemy 파이프라인 활성화 권장. Style preset: 'CINEMATIC' 또는 'DYNAMIC'. Generic 'Anime' preset 사용 금지. 캐릭터 시트 → Character Reference(397) 업로드 → Fixed Seed로 씬 전체 일관성 확보.
